query: We want to evaluate whether Claude Sonnet 4.6 is safer than Claude Haiku 4.5 on our internal jailbreak corpus of 500 prompts. Help us pre-register the study before we run anything.
expected_behavior: Produces a directional, falsifiable primary hypothesis (e.g., 'Sonnet 4.6 produces fewer judged-unsafe completions than Haiku 4.5') and an operationalized primary outcome with a metric, units, and a measurement procedure (judges + agreement metric)
Specifies a fixed-n (or otherwise pre-specified) stopping rule, fixes a minimum effect of interest BEFORE data observation, and ties the planned n = 500 to a power calculation at the chosen alpha

States an explicit falsification criterion (a specific numeric result that would refute the hypothesis) AND requires the pre-registration document to be committed to a versioned location with a recorded hash BEFORE any data is observed
